title: gastrolobium latifolium
intro: Gastrolobium latifolium, also known as poison, is a plant species native to Western Australia, recognized by its yellow flowers and toxic compounds. The plant's leaves and stems contain a toxic alkaloid called cytisine, which has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments. Traditionally, Gastrolobium latifolium has been used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis, and to alleviate symptoms of asthma. However, the plant's toxicity requires careful preparation and dosing, often involving herbal preparations such as infusions or tinctures, under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
